title: uputreg
text: uputreg Copy a long integer value into the specified general variable ($1 ... $99). Explain this code long uputreg (shortregNbr, longvalue); Copy Parameters regNbr Number of the Uniface general variable; must be in the range 1 to 99. value Long integer value to put into the variable. Values can be from -2147483648 to 2147483647. Return Values The value of the value parameter. Use Library: yrtl Description uputreg copies the specified long integer value, which can range from -2147483648 to 2147483647 (or -231 to 231 -1), to the Uniface general variable (also known as dollar register) with the specified number regNbr , which can range from 1 to 99. There is no check on the validity of regNbr ; if it is out of range, a crash is likely to result. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT long putnum () { (void)uputreg(1, -2147483648); /* Lowest value possible */ } Copy Related Links ugetreg Get the integer value of a Uniface general variable ($1 ... $99). uputregf Copy a double-precision floating-point value into the specified general variable ($1 ... $99). uputregs Copy a string value to the specified general variable ($1 ... $99).
